exemplar.title: How does Dudu Busani Dube in the novel, The Wife, portray women suffering in societies under the facade of a happy marriage?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Dudu Busani Dube in the novel, The Wife, portray women suffering in societies under the facade of a happy marriage?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Thematic focus is clear and sustained throughout, with essay headings reinforcing the central inquiry.
Research question is explicitly stated, wellâconnected to discussion, and revisited effectively.
Headings and transitions maintain coherence and purpose, demonstrating a strong alignment between focus and methods.
weaknesses: Methodology is implied rather than explicitly described; lacks a dedicated section explaining source selection.
Range of sources is moderate, with several popular web sources rather than a broader scholarly base.
Some phrasing is occasionally wordy or imprecise, which slightly detracts from methodological clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation consistently communicates its focus on womenâs suffering under marital faÃ§ades and maintains a strong connection to the research question. While the methodological approach (close reading supplemented by socioâhistorical sources) is generally effective, the absence of an explicit methodology section and a moderate range of sources limit the strandâs performance to the upper end of band 3â4, warranting 4 marks.

strengths: Demonstrates coherent understanding of the novelâs plot and relevant socioâcultural contexts (e.g., brideâprice customs, domestic violence data).
Effectively integrates secondary sources to support key points, such as using Wood & Jewkes on abuse prevalence.
Shows insightful contextual analysis, for example, the overview of precolonial female power and brideâprice symbolism.
weaknesses: Reliance on nonâacademic web sources and occasional factual vagueness prevent a fully âexcellentâ standard.
Inconsistent use of terminology (e.g., character names) and sometimes loose definitions reduce precision.
Several contextual claims lack inâtext citations; inconsistent referencing undermines source credibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work displays good knowledge of the primary text and associated concepts, with clear applications of sources, but occasional imprecision, inconsistent terminology, and some weak source choices limit its performance to the middle of band 3â4. A score of 3 reflects solid yet improvable knowledge and use of concepts.

strengths: A coherent argument is sustained throughout, linking textual analysis to wider social romanticization of abuse.
Research draws from a relevant bibliography of secondary sources that inform the discussion.
Close readings (e.g., courting scene, blade metaphor) are wellâinterpreted and deepen the critical argument.
weaknesses: Evaluation is uneven: sections sometimes revert to summary or broad commentary without close textual linkage.
Counterâarguments and deeper critical reflection are limited, and unsupported assertions occasionally appear.
The conclusion reiterates earlier language rather than offering novel insights or critical weigh-ups.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall within the 4â6 band: research is good but mixed in scholarly rigor, analysis is generally effective but inconsistent, and evaluation is only partially critical. Performance sits at the lower end of this band, meriting 4 out of 12.

strengths: Logical essay structure with clear sectional headings, a contents page, and a coherent progression from introduction to conclusion.
Basic layout conventions (bold headings, bibliography) facilitate readability.
Sections and subheadings are appropriately organized, aiding navigation.
weaknesses: Key formal requirements are missing: no word count, abstract, or separate RPPF document.
Page numbering is inconsistent and citation style contains errors.
Heading levels and formatting are uneven, with some fragments and spacing issues.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the structure is clearly appropriate, significant formal lapses (missing word count and abstract, inconsistent pagination, citation errors) limit the presentation to the lower end of band 1â2, justifying 2 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The student employs a recognizable literary-analytical method (close reading of key scenes) and supplements it with socio-historical secondary sources.  A bibliography is included and in-text parenthetical references appear, indicating informed selection.  
However, the range is moderate: only one primary text is treated and several web sources are popular rather than scholarly; citation style is inconsistent.  There is no explicit explanation of how sources were selected or evaluated for reliability.  These limitations keep the strand in the middle of the 3â4 band.

Basic layout conventionsâcontents table, bold headings, bibliographyâare applied, and some page markers appear in braces.  However, formal EE formatting has notable lapses: no explicit word count, inconsistent page numbering, and citation style errors.  Consequently, only âsomeâ layout considerations are met, fitting the 1â2 band (upper end).

The bibliography lists over a dozen secondary sources spanning journals, encyclopaedias and NGO sites.  Most are germane to gender roles or domestic violence, directly informing the research question.  
While scholarly depth is mixed, the majority of material remains relevant and is drawn on in discussion, meeting the descriptors for âgoodâ research.  Some weaker popular sources prevent entry to the 7â9 band, so a mid-range 5 within 4â6 is appropriate.

The introduction states a clear thematic focus on âwomenâs suffering under the faÃ§ade of a happy marriageâ and immediately anchors this to Dudu Busani Dubeâs novel The Wife.  Throughout the essay headings (âRoles of Women in Africa,â âFacade of a perfect marriage,â etc.) keep the line of inquiry visible and coherent.
â¢  The student maintains this focus consistently, returning to the guiding idea of social perception versus lived abuse in most body sections.
â¢  Some phrasing is occasionally wordy or imprecise, but overall the purpose and direction are unmistakable and sustained, matching the descriptors for the 3â4 band at its upper end.

An overall argument is evident: the novel exposes the social romanticisation of abuse.  The student structures discussion around stages of the relationship and attempts periodic evaluation (e.g., whether society or individual psychology traps Hlomu).  
Yet evaluation is uneven; sections sometimes rely on assertions and the conclusion largely restates earlier points rather than critically weighing findings.  This reflects âgood argument development with partially critical evaluationâ, meriting a 4 in the 4â6 band.

Analysis moves beyond description by interrogating specific scenes (taxi-rank courtship, first assault, hair cutting) and linking them to societal norms.  Claims are usually supported with textual quotation or secondary evidence.  
Nonetheless, reasoning sometimes drifts into summary or broad cultural commentary without close textual linkage, and counter-arguments are rare.  Support is âmostlyâ rather than âfullyâ sustained, situating the work at the lower end of the 4â6 band.

The essay follows the required EE structure: title page, contents, clear sectional headings, introduction, body chapters, conclusion, and bibliography.  Logical progression from broad context to detailed textual analysis is maintained.
Minor omissions (no abstract, RPPF separate document not supplied) and occasional pagination irregularities prevent a top score, but the structure remains clearly appropriate overall.

The essay demonstrates solid knowledge of both the chosen novel and wider socio-cultural discourse.  Summaries of plot, contextualisation of bride-price customs, and references to domestic-violence data show coherent understanding.  
Sources are generally integrated to support points (e.g., Wood & Jewkes to substantiate prevalence of abuse).  Occasional factual vagueness and reliance on non-academic webpages prevent the work from reaching a fully âexcellentâ standard, but overall quality fits comfortably at the top of the 3â4 band.

Key conceptsâpatriarchy, Stockholm syndrome, bride-price, monologue narrationâare employed and mostly accurate.  Literary terminology (characterisation, symbolism) appears, though sometimes loosely defined.  
Inconsistencies (e.g., interchangeable use of âMahele/Mqheleâ; occasional informal phrasing) reduce precision and consistency.  This places performance solidly but not exceptionally within the 3â4 band.

The research question is explicitly formulated (âHow does Dudu Busani Dube â¦ portray women suffering â¦?â).  It is specific to one primary text and a clearly-defined social lens, demonstrating an adequate level of focus.  
The later chapters repeatedly tie claims back to this question (e.g., analysis of courting scenes, bride-price episode, hair-cut symbol), showing a functional, if not always nuanced, connection between question and discussion.  Minor breadth issues remainâcontextual excursions into colonial history sometimes over-expandâbut the linkage is largely maintained, justifying a high 3â4 band award.

annotations.comment: When quoting dialogue, include a page reference immediately after the quotation.
Inconsistent character name (âMoneleâ vs âMgheleâ); ensure correct spelling throughout.
A word count is missing; include it under the title to meet EE formatting requirements.
The title is clearly stated at the start, satisfying the formatting requirement for a title page.
The research question is stated clearly and aligns with the investigationâs focus.
The contents page is well organized with clear sectional headings, aiding navigation.
Page numbers are omitted from the contents entries; include them for reader clarity.
The introduction provides broad context on African literature but lacks in-text citations for some claims.
Several general statements about African authors are presented without referencing specific works or dates.
The research question restatement on page 4 is succinct and keeps the focus tight on the novelâs thematic exploration.
Break the research question into one line for precision and to avoid splitting across lines.
Methodology is not explicitly described; add a brief section outlining source selection and analytical approach.
The heading âThe long reign of colonialismâ¦â transitions logically into analysis of its impact on women.
The <math> tag appears in headings and page numbers due to OCR; replace these with standard text.
Good close reading of the courting scene links textual detail to wider social norms.
Personal anecdote about reader response on X.com is informal; reframe in academic tone or relocate to reflective commentary.
The contextual definition of âSouthern African cultureâ would benefit from a precise citation or source.
The overview of precolonial female power demonstrates good contextual understanding.
Citations like â(Narayan)â lack year and page; complete each parenthetical reference for consistency.
Use consistent heading levels (e.g., H3 for subheads) to improve structural clarity.
The discussion of fatherâdaughter attachment draws on Dinero et al.; ground the link more explicitly.
Sentence fragment at top of page (âcountry not at warâ) needs revision for full clarity.
Insightful analysis of bride-price symbolism contextualizes gendered power dynamics.
The metaphor of the blade is interpreted effectively; it deepens the argument on societal roles.
Parenthetical citations in analysis (e.g., â(Jones)â) need consistent formatting including publication year.
Strong evaluation of Hlomuâs emancipation trajectory shows analytical depth.
Conclusion effectively synthesizes key arguments but repeats earlier language; focus on novel insights.
No reflection or RPPF section is provided; include a separate reflection to demonstrate planning and decision-making.
Works Cited entries display mixed citation styles; apply one consistent style across all sources.
Bibliography list is not alphabetized; re-order entries for standard reference conventions.
Diverse sources on page 16 demonstrate good breadth, but academic peer-reviewed journals could be increased.
Ensure page numbering prints consistently rather than relying on OCR artifacts.
